title: PipeShare BASE file explorer scope fix
description:
On pipeshare.live the file explorer could load an empty Wasabi prefix when stale SaaS tenant URL params (`?clientId=tenant-*`) or admin deep-link overrides pointed at tenant virtualbox storage instead of shared `portal-users/8` where all BASE files live.

OVH verification: `GET /api/files/tree?clientId=portal-users&jobId=8` returns 4581 files; `tenant-mike-strickland/1` returns zero. The frontend now ignores tenant URL scope on BASE, strips those params at boot, resolves session/bootstrap defaults, and retries tree fetch when the first scope is empty.

Who is affected: PipeShare on the private BASE server (pipeshare.live / OVH). SaaS tenants are unchanged.
changeLog: Ignore tenant-* URL clientId on pipeshare.live — use portal-users/{jobId}.
Strip stale tenant query params from BASE URLs at every boot.
Admins without explicit scope fall back to deployment bootstrap defaults (portal-users/8).
Skip hydrating empty cached trees; retry BASE scope when first tree fetch is empty.
Do not treat stale tenant clientId in local session as Wasabi scope on BASE.
